title: Connect to Your Federated Database Instance

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To complete this part of the tutorial, you will need to have completed
the following:

- **Part 1:** [deploying a federated database instance](gst-deploy-adf)

- **Part 2:** [Configure Connection for Your Federated Database Instance](config-connection)

sections.heading: Step 1: Click the  button for the federated database instance that you want to connect to.
Step 2: Choose the type of connection.
sections.body:
You can choose one of the following:

- **Private Endpoint** if you already configured endpoints
  and want to use the connection string for your endpoint.

  > **Note:** If you don't have a configured private endpoint to select in
  > the **Connect** modal dropdown, the dropdown displays
  > the standard connection string.

- **Standard Connection** if you want to use your
  deployment's standard connection string.

sections.heading: Step 3: For  connection type, select the configured endpoint to connect to from the dropdown.
Step 4: Add a connection IP address and create a database user if you haven't already done so and click .
Step 5: Choose your connection method.
sections.body:
You can connect using any of the following:

- [MongoDB Drivers](drivers)

- [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass)

- [mongosh](mongodb-shell)

- [VS Code](mongodb-vscode)

- [MongoSQL](atlas/data-federation/query/query-with-sql)

> **Note:** To run the sample queries, choose [mongosh](mongodb-shell).

sections.heading: Step 6: Connect to your federated database instance.
sections.body:
To learn more about connecting to your federated database instance using your selected
connection method, see:

- [Driver](connect-via-driver)

- [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass)

- [MongoDB Shell](connect-mongo-shell)

- [Connect to a Cluster via VS Code](mongodb-for-vscode)

- [Connect Your BI Tool](sql-interface/connect)

> **Note:** Only one user can authenticate on a connection to a federated database instance at any
> given time. If a user authenticates and then runs the `db.auth()`
> command, Data Federation replaces the previous user's permissions with the new
> user's permissions.
>
> The [connectionStatus](manual/reference/command/connectionStatus)
> command shows only the newly authenticated user in the
> `authenticatedUsers` output field.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body:
Now that you're connected to your federated database instance using [mongosh](mongodb-shell), proceed to
Run Queries Against Your federated database instance.
